name: devzero
description: A Pulumi provider for managing DevZero infrastructure resources.

token.description: The DevZero API token for authentication.

url.description: The DevZero API base URL. Defaults to https://dakr.devzero.io

alias.description: Well-known alias for the AMI family. Example: 'al2@latest' or 'bottlerocket@latest'.

owner.description: AWS account ID or alias that owns the AMI. Example: '123456789012' or 'amazon'.

ssmParameter.description: SSM parameter path that stores the AMI ID. Example: '/aws/service/eks/optimized-ami/1.29/amazon-linux-2/recommended/image_id'.

tags.description: Map of AWS tags used to select AMIs. Example: {"my-org/ami": "approved"}.

amiFamily.description: AMI family shorthand used when no amiSelectorTerms are specified. One of: 'AL2', 'AL2023', 'Bottlerocket', 'Windows2019', 'Windows2022'. Example: 'AL2'.

amiSelectorTerms.description: Selectors for the AMIs used to launch nodes. Example: [{alias: "al2@latest"}].

associatePublicIpAddress.description: Whether to assign a public IP address to provisioned nodes. Example: false.

blockDeviceMappings.description: EBS block device mappings for nodes. Example: [{deviceName: "/dev/xvda", rootVolume: true, ebs: {volumeSize: "50Gi", volumeType: "gp3"}}].

capacityReservationSelectorTerms.description: Selectors for EC2 capacity reservations to prioritize. Example: [{tags: {"aws:ec2:fleet-id": "fleet-123"}}].

context.description: Additional EC2 launch template context ARN for advanced customization. Example: 'arn:aws:ec2:us-east-1:123456789012:launch-template/lt-0abc123'.

detailedMonitoring.description: Enable detailed (1-minute interval) CloudWatch monitoring for instances. Example: false.

instanceProfile.description: IAM instance profile name to use directly (alternative to Role). Example: 'KarpenterNodeInstanceProfile-my-cluster'.

instanceStorePolicy.description: Policy for handling NVMe instance store volumes. One of: 'INSTANCE_STORE_POLICY_RAID0'. Example: 'INSTANCE_STORE_POLICY_RAID0'.

kubelet.description: Kubelet configuration overrides applied to all nodes in this class.

role.description: IAM role name assigned to nodes (Karpenter creates the instance profile). Example: 'KarpenterNodeRole-my-cluster'.

securityGroupSelectorTerms.description: Selectors for security groups attached to provisioned nodes. Example: [{tags: {"karpenter.sh/discovery": "my-cluster"}}].

subnetSelectorTerms.description: Selectors for the subnets nodes will be launched into. Example: [{tags: {"karpenter.sh/discovery": "my-cluster"}}].

tags.description: AWS tags applied to all resources (instances, volumes, ENIs) created by this node class. Example: {"environment": "production", "team": "platform"}.

userData.description: Custom user data script merged into the node launch template (base64 or plain text). Example: '#!/bin/bash\necho hello'.

allowedUnsafeSysctls.description: Unsafe sysctl patterns permitted on nodes. Example: ["net.ipv4.*", "net.ipv6.*"].

containerLogMaxFiles.description: Maximum number of container log files to retain per container. Example: 5.

containerLogMaxSize.description: Maximum container log file size before rotation. Example: '10Mi'.

cpuCfsQuota.description: Whether to enforce CPU CFS quota limits for containers. Example: true.

cpuManagerPolicy.description: CPU manager policy for CPU pinning. One of: 'none', 'static'. Example: 'static'.

imageGcHighThresholdPercent.description: Disk usage % that triggers image garbage collection. Example: 85.

imageGcLowThresholdPercent.description: Disk usage % at which image GC stops freeing space. Example: 70.

podPidsLimit.description: Maximum number of process IDs allowed per pod. Example: 1024.

topologyManagerPolicy.description: Topology manager policy for NUMA-aware workloads. One of: 'none', 'best-effort', 'restricted', 'single-numa-node'. Example: 'none'.

fipsMode.description: FIPS 140-2 compliance mode for the node. One of: 'Enabled', 'Disabled'. Example: 'Disabled'.

imageFamily.description: Azure node image family. One of: 'AzureLinux', 'Ubuntu2204'. Example: 'AzureLinux'.

kubelet.description: Kubelet configuration overrides for Azure nodes.

maxPods.description: Maximum pods per node, overrides the AKS cluster default. Example: 110.

osDiskSizeGb.description: OS disk size in GB. Example: 128.

tags.description: Azure tags applied to all resources created by this node class. Example: {"environment": "production"}.

vnetSubnetId.description: Azure VNet subnet resource ID where nodes will be placed. Example: '/subscriptions/sub-id/resourceGroups/my-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/my-vnet/subnets/nodesubnet'.

deleteOnTermination.description: Whether to delete the EBS volume when the instance terminates. Example: true.

encrypted.description: Whether to encrypt the EBS volume. Example: true.

iops.description: IOPS to provision for io1/io2 volume types. Example: 3000.

kmsKeyId.description: KMS key ID or ARN used to encrypt the volume. Example: 'arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/mrk-abc123'.

snapshotId.description: EBS snapshot ID to restore the volume from. Example: 'snap-0a1b2c3d4e5f'.

throughput.description: Throughput in MiB/s for gp3 volumes (125-1000). Example: 125.

volumeInitializationRate.description: Rate in MiB/s for initializing volumes from snapshots. Example: 300.

volumeSize.description: Volume size with unit suffix. Example: '20Gi'.

volumeType.description: EBS volume type. One of: 'gp2', 'gp3', 'io1', 'io2', 'st1', 'sc1'. Example: 'gp3'.

deviceName.description: Device name to map the volume to. Example: '/dev/xvda' (root on AL2), '/dev/sdb' (data volume).

ebs.description: EBS volume configuration for this device mapping.

rootVolume.description: Whether this mapping is for the root (boot) volume. Example: true.

ownerId.description: AWS account ID that owns the capacity reservation. Example: '123456789012'.

tags.description: Map of AWS tags used to select capacity reservations. Example: {"aws:ec2:fleet-id": "fleet-123"}.

duration.description: How long the budget window stays active after the cron fires. Example: '8h'.

nodes.description: Maximum nodes that may be disrupted simultaneously, as an absolute count or percentage. Example: '10%' or '5'.

reasons.description: Disruption reasons this budget applies to. Valid values: 'Underutilized', 'Empty', 'Drifted'. Example: ["Underutilized", "Empty"].

schedule.description: Cron schedule (5-field UTC) during which this budget is active. Example: '0 9 * * mon-fri' (weekday business hours).

budgets.description: Disruption budgets controlling how many nodes can be disrupted simultaneously. Example: [{reasons: ["Underutilized"], nodes: "10%"}].

consolidateAfter.description: Duration to wait after a node becomes empty before consolidating. Example: '30s'.

consolidationPolicy.description: When to consolidate nodes. One of: 'WhenEmpty', 'WhenEmptyOrUnderutilized'. Example: 'WhenEmptyOrUnderutilized'.

expireAfter.description: Duration after which provisioned nodes are replaced regardless of load. Example: '720h' (30 days).

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Grace period in seconds before forcefully terminating a draining node. Example: 600.

ttlSecondsAfterEmpty.description: Seconds before an empty node is terminated (deprecated; prefer consolidateAfter). Example: 30.

maxReplicaChangePercent.description: Maximum percentage change in replica count per recommendation cycle. Example: 50.0 allows up to 50% change.

maxReplicas.description: Maximum number of replicas to scale up to. Example: 10.

minDataPoints.description: Minimum data points required before a recommendation is emitted. Example: 20.

minReplicas.description: Minimum number of replicas to maintain. Example: 2.

primaryMetric.description: Primary metric for HPA decisions. One of: 'cpu', 'memory', 'gpu', 'network_ingress', 'network_egress'. Example: 'cpu'.

targetUtilization.description: Target utilization ratio (0-1) for the primary metric. Example: 0.7 targets 70% utilization.

clusterDns.description: DNS server IP addresses passed to kubelet. Example: ["10.96.0.10"].

cpuCfsQuota.description: Whether to enforce CPU CFS quota limits for containers. Example: true.

evictionHard.description: Hard eviction thresholds — pods are evicted immediately when crossed. Example: {"memory.available": "100Mi", "nodefs.available": "10%"}.

evictionMaxPodGracePeriod.description: Maximum pod termination grace period (seconds) during eviction. Example: 90.

evictionSoft.description: Soft eviction thresholds — eviction begins after the grace period expires. Example: {"memory.available": "200Mi"}.

evictionSoftGracePeriod.description: Grace period before acting on a soft eviction threshold. Example: {"memory.available": "90s"}.

imageGcHighThresholdPercent.description: Disk usage % that triggers image garbage collection. Example: 85.

imageGcLowThresholdPercent.description: Disk usage % at which image GC stops freeing space. Example: 70.

kubeReserved.description: Resources reserved for Kubernetes system components (kubelet, kube-proxy). Example: {"cpu": "100m", "memory": "100Mi"}.

maxPods.description: Maximum number of pods allowed per node. Example: 110.

podsPerCore.description: Maximum pods per CPU core; multiplied by node core count for effective max. Example: 10.

systemReserved.description: Resources reserved for OS system daemons (not Kubernetes). Example: {"cpu": "100m", "memory": "100Mi"}.

matchExpressions.description: List of label selector requirements joined by AND.

matchLabels.description: Map of label key-value pairs that must all match exactly.

key.description: The label key that the selector applies to.

operator.description: Operator relating the key and values. One of: 'In', 'NotIn', 'Exists', 'DoesNotExist'.

values.description: Array of string values. Required for 'In' and 'NotIn' operators.

httpEndpoint.description: Enable or disable the EC2 instance metadata endpoint. One of: 'enabled', 'disabled'. Example: 'enabled'.

httpProtocolIpv6.description: Enable IPv6 for the metadata endpoint. One of: 'enabled', 'disabled'. Example: 'disabled'.

httpPutResponseHopLimit.description: HTTP PUT response hop limit for metadata requests (1-64). Set to 1 to block pod-level IMDS access. Example: 1.

httpTokens.description: Whether to require IMDSv2 session tokens (recommended). One of: 'optional', 'required'. Example: 'required'.

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern to match workload names.

nodeclassYaml.description:
Raw YAML for a complete Karpenter NodeClass resource — use as an escape hatch when structured fields are insufficient. Example: 'apiVersion: karpenter.k8s.aws/v1
kind: EC2NodeClass
metadata:
  name: default
...'.

nodepoolYaml.description:
Raw YAML for a complete Karpenter NodePool resource — use as an escape hatch when structured fields are insufficient. Example: 'apiVersion: karpenter.sh/v1
kind: NodePool
metadata:
  name: default
...'.

cpu.description: Maximum total vCPUs that may be provisioned across all nodes in this pool. Example: '1000'.

memory.description: Maximum total memory that may be provisioned across all nodes. Example: '1000Gi'.

id.description: Explicit AWS security group ID. Example: 'sg-0a1b2c3d4e5f'.

name.description: Security group name filter (exact match). Example: 'my-cluster-node-sg'.

tags.description: Map of AWS tags used to select security groups. Example: {"karpenter.sh/discovery": "my-cluster"}.

id.description: Explicit AWS subnet ID (use instead of tags for a fixed subnet). Example: 'subnet-0a1b2c3d4e5f'.

tags.description: Map of AWS tags used to select subnets. Example: {"karpenter.sh/discovery": "my-cluster"}.

key.description: Taint key to apply to provisioned nodes. Example: 'dedicated'.

value.description: Taint value associated with the key. Example: 'gpu'.

adjustReqEvenIfNotSet.description: Recommend requests even when the workload has no existing requests set. Default: false.

enabled.description: Enable vertical scaling for this resource type. Example: true

limitMultiplier.description: Multiplier applied to the request to derive the resource limit. Example: 1.5 sets limit to 150% of request.

limitsAdjustmentEnabled.description: Whether to also adjust resource limits alongside requests. Example: true.

limitsRemovalEnabled.description: Actively remove limits from workloads (CPU axis only — memory limits removal is not supported). Takes precedence over limitsAdjustmentEnabled when set. Default: false.

maxRequest.description: Maximum resource request in millicores (CPU) or bytes (memory/GPU). Example: 4000 (= 4 CPU cores), 1073741824 (= 1Gi memory).

maxScaleDownPercent.description: Maximum percentage decrease allowed in a single recommendation cycle. Example: 20.0 allows up to 20% decrease. Default: 1.0.

maxScaleUpPercent.description: Maximum percentage increase allowed in a single recommendation cycle. Example: 50.0 allows up to 50% increase. Default: 1000.0.

minDataPoints.description: Minimum number of data points required before a recommendation is emitted. Example: 20 (default).

minRequest.description: Minimum resource request in millicores (CPU) or bytes (memory/GPU). Example: 100 (= 100m CPU), 134217728 (= 128Mi memory).

overheadMultiplier.description: Multiplier applied on top of the recommendation to add headroom. Example: 1.15 adds 15% overhead.

targetPercentile.description: Percentile of usage data used as the recommendation target (0-1). Example: 0.95 targets the 95th percentile.

token.description: Authentication token for the cluster. Rotated automatically if empty on update (e.g. after import).

aws.description: AWS-specific EC2NodeClass configuration (subnets, AMIs, IAM role, EBS, etc.).

azure.description: Azure-specific AKSNodeClass configuration (VNet subnet, OS disk, image family, etc.).

capacityTypes.description: Capacity purchasing types. Valid values: 'spot', 'on-demand', 'reserved'. Example: {in: ["spot", "on-demand"]}.

description.description: Free-form description of the node policy. Example: 'Spot instance policy for production batch workloads'.

disruption.description: Karpenter disruption policy controlling consolidation, expiry, and budgets.

instanceCategories.description: Filter instances by category letter (cloud-specific). Example: {in: ["m", "c", "r"]} for AWS, {in: ["D", "E"]} for Azure.

instanceCpus.description: Filter instances by vCPU count. Example: {in: ["4", "8", "16"]}.

instanceGenerations.description: Filter instances by generation number. Example: {in: ["2", "3"]}.

instanceHypervisors.description: Filter instances by hypervisor type. Example: {in: ["nitro"]}.

instanceSizes.description: Filter instances by size label. Example: {in: ["large", "xlarge", "2xlarge"]}.

instanceTypes.description: Explicitly allow specific instance types. Example: {in: ["m5.large", "c6i.large"]}.

labels.description: Labels applied to all provisioned nodes. Example: {"team": "backend", "env": "prod"}.

limits.description: Resource limits on total capacity managed by this policy. Example: {cpu: "1000", memory: "1000Gi"}.

name.description: Human-friendly name for the node policy. Example: 'prod-spot-policy'.

nodeClassName.description: Override name for the generated Karpenter NodeClass resource. Example: 'prod-aws-nodeclass'.

nodePoolName.description: Override name for the generated Karpenter NodePool resource. Example: 'prod-spot-nodepool'.

raw.description: Raw Karpenter YAML for full NodePool/NodeClass customization — use only when structured fields are insufficient.

taints.description: Taints applied to provisioned nodes to control pod scheduling. Example: [{key: "dedicated", value: "gpu", effect: "NoSchedule"}].

weight.description: Priority weight; higher values take precedence when multiple policies match. Example: 100.

zones.description: Availability zones where nodes may be provisioned. Example: {in: ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b"]}.

clusterIds.description: Cluster IDs where this node policy applies.

enabled.description: Whether this target is active. Defaults to true.

policyId.description: Node policy ID this target is attached to.

actionTriggers.description: When to apply recommendations. Valid values: 'on_detection', 'on_schedule'. Example: ["on_detection"].

cooldownMinutes.description: Minimum minutes to wait between consecutive recommendation applications. Example: 300 (5 h, default).

cronSchedule.description: Cron expression for scheduled application (5-field UTC format). Required when actionTriggers includes 'on_schedule'. Example: '0 2 * * *' (daily at 2 am UTC).

defragmentationSchedule.description: Cron expression for background node defragmentation. Example: '0 3 * * 0' (weekly Sunday at 3 am).

description.description: Free-form description of the policy. Example: 'VPA policy for production workloads'.

detectionTriggers.description: Events that trigger a new recommendation. Valid values: 'pod_creation', 'pod_update', 'pod_reschedule'. Example: ["pod_creation", "pod_reschedule"].

driftDeltaPercent.description: Percentage change from the baseline recommendation that triggers a VPA refresh. Example: 20.0.

enablePmaxProtection.description: Raise requests to cover observed peak usage when the peak/recommendation ratio exceeds pmaxRatioThreshold. Default: false.

gpuVerticalScaling.description: Vertical scaling configuration for GPU cores. Uses the same fields as cpuVerticalScaling; units are GPU cores (millicores).

gpuVramVerticalScaling.description: Vertical scaling configuration for GPU VRAM. Uses the same fields as cpuVerticalScaling; units are bytes.

hysteresisVsTarget.description: Dead-band ratio around the HPA target to suppress oscillation between VPA and HPA. Example: 0.1 (10% band).

liveMigrationEnabled.description: Allow live pod migration when applying recommendations without restart. Example: false.

loopbackPeriodSeconds.description: Seconds of historical usage data considered per recommendation. Example: 86400 (24 h, default).

minChangePercent.description: Minimum relative change (0-1) required before a recommendation is applied globally. Example: 0.2 means 20% change needed (default).

minDataPoints.description: Global minimum number of usage data points needed before any recommendation is emitted. Example: 15 (default).

minVpaWindowDataPoints.description: Minimum data points inside the VPA analysis window before a recommendation is generated. Example: 30 (default).

name.description: Human-friendly name for the policy. Example: 'production-vpa-policy'.

pmaxRatioThreshold.description: Peak-to-recommendation ratio above which pmax protection activates. Example: 3.0 (default) — triggers when peak is 3× the recommendation.

schedulerPlugins.description: Kubernetes scheduler plugins to activate for this policy. Example: ["binpacking"].

stabilityCvMax.description: Maximum coefficient of variation (stddev/mean) for a workload to be considered stable enough for VPA. Example: 0.3.

startupPeriodSeconds.description: Seconds after workload start to exclude from usage data (avoids cold-start spikes). Example: 300 (5 min).

actionTriggers.description: When to apply recommendations. Valid values: 'on_detection', 'on_schedule'. Example: ["on_detection"].

cooldownMinutes.description: Minimum minutes to wait between consecutive recommendation applications. Example: 300 (5 h, default).

cronSchedule.description: Cron expression for scheduled application (5-field UTC format). Required when actionTriggers includes 'on_schedule'. Example: '0 2 * * *' (daily at 2 am UTC).

defragmentationSchedule.description: Cron expression for background node defragmentation. Example: '0 3 * * 0' (weekly Sunday at 3 am).

description.description: Free-form description of the policy. Example: 'VPA policy for production workloads'.

detectionTriggers.description: Events that trigger a new recommendation. Valid values: 'pod_creation', 'pod_update', 'pod_reschedule'. Example: ["pod_creation", "pod_reschedule"].

driftDeltaPercent.description: Percentage change from the baseline recommendation that triggers a VPA refresh. Example: 20.0.

enablePmaxProtection.description: Raise requests to cover observed peak usage when the peak/recommendation ratio exceeds pmaxRatioThreshold. Default: false.

gpuVerticalScaling.description: Vertical scaling configuration for GPU cores. Uses the same fields as cpuVerticalScaling; units are GPU cores (millicores).

gpuVramVerticalScaling.description: Vertical scaling configuration for GPU VRAM. Uses the same fields as cpuVerticalScaling; units are bytes.

hysteresisVsTarget.description: Dead-band ratio around the HPA target to suppress oscillation between VPA and HPA. Example: 0.1 (10% band).

liveMigrationEnabled.description: Allow live pod migration when applying recommendations without restart. Example: false.

loopbackPeriodSeconds.description: Seconds of historical usage data considered per recommendation. Example: 86400 (24 h, default).

minChangePercent.description: Minimum relative change (0-1) required before a recommendation is applied globally. Example: 0.2 means 20% change needed (default).

minDataPoints.description: Global minimum number of usage data points needed before any recommendation is emitted. Example: 15 (default).

minVpaWindowDataPoints.description: Minimum data points inside the VPA analysis window before a recommendation is generated. Example: 30 (default).

name.description: Human-friendly name for the policy. Example: 'production-vpa-policy'.

pmaxRatioThreshold.description: Peak-to-recommendation ratio above which pmax protection activates. Example: 3.0 (default) — triggers when peak is 3× the recommendation.

schedulerPlugins.description: Kubernetes scheduler plugins to activate for this policy. Example: ["binpacking"].

stabilityCvMax.description: Maximum coefficient of variation (stddev/mean) for a workload to be considered stable enough for VPA. Example: 0.3.

startupPeriodSeconds.description: Seconds after workload start to exclude from usage data (avoids cold-start spikes). Example: 300 (5 min).

clusterIds.description: Cluster IDs where this target applies.

kindFilter.description: Restrict matching to specific Kubernetes kinds (e.g. Deployment, Pod).

nodeGroupNames.description: Restrict matching to specific node groups by name.

policyId.description: Workload policy ID this target is attached to.

priority.description: Evaluation priority; higher values take precedence when targets overlap.

workloadNames.description: Explicit list of workload names to include.

cloudProvider.description: Optional cloud provider filter. One of: 'AWS', 'GCP', 'AKS', 'OCI'.

liveness.description: Controls liveness filtering: IGNORE, PREFER_LIVE, or REQUIRE_LIVE.

name.description: The cluster name to look up.

teamId.description: The team ID to search within.

clusterId.description: The ID of the cluster matching the given team and name.
